---
title: "Horseweed (Erigeron canadensis)"
slug: "erigeron-canadensis"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621856279/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Horseweed"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Asterales"
tax_family: "Asteraceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Asterales", "Asteraceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621856279/medium.jpg" alt="Horseweed" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/jimw3">
jimw3</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341578511">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Asterales
-   **Family:** Asteraceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=76907)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in disturbed soils, roadsides, fields, and gardens.

## Natural history notes

An upright, weedy wildflower characterized by its narrow leaves and tall stems topped with many small, inconspicuous flower heads. In Florida, it is commonly found in disturbed areas such as fields, roadsides, and vacant lots. It grows during the warmer months and produces abundant wind-dispersed seeds, allowing it to spread widely.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/76907-Erigeron-canadensis)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/genus/352)
